clusters.title: 'A bit of a mixed verdict': Musk misled Twitter shareholders, jury finds
clusters.neutral_headline: Elon Musk Found Liable For Misleading Twitter Investors

items.title: 'A bit of a mixed verdict': Musk misled Twitter shareholders, jury finds
items.standfirst: Tech multi-billionaire Elon Musk could be forced to pay billions of dollars in damages after a federal jury found that the world's richest man had misled Twitter shareholders while purchasing the social media platform. Musk was found to have posted false statements to drive down the company's share price. FRANCE 24 correspondent Wassim Cornet reports from Los Angeles.
items.content: Tech multi-billionaire Elon Musk could be forced to pay billions of dollars in damages after a federal jury found that the world's richest man had misled Twitter shareholders while purchasing the social media platform. Musk was found to have posted false statements to drive down the company's share price. FRANCE 24 correspondent Wassim Cornet reports from Los Angeles.

items.standfirst: President says US ‘getting very close to meeting our objectives’; missiles fired at joint US-UK military base in Indian Ocean but neither hit, reports sayCircling back now to Diego Garcia, Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean – but neither of them hit, according to news reports citing US officials.The Wall Street Journal said one of the missiles failed in flight, and that a US warship fired an SM-3 interceptor at the other, citing two US officials. items.title: Middle East war live: Iran targets UK-US military base Diego Garcia, US and Israel strike Natanz nuclear site
items.standfirst: The US and Israel once again struck Iran’s Natanz nuclear facility Saturday, the Islamic Republic’s atomic energy organisation said. Iran earlier fired two ballistic missiles at the US-UK military base Diego Garcia in the ‌Indian Ocean, the semi-official Mehr ‌news agency reported. Although neither missile hit its target, the launch suggests Tehran’s missiles have a longer ranger than previously thought. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Iranian among two charged over alleged attempt to enter UK nuclear submarine base
items.standfirst: Police say two people tried to enter Faslane base in Scotland, home to core of UK’s submarine fleet and Trident nuclear weaponsTwo people have been charged, one of them Iranian, after they allegedly tried to enter HM Naval Base Clyde in Scotland, which houses the UK’s nuclear Trident submarines.A 34-year-old man and a 31-year-old woman were charged after the incident at the base, which is known as Faslane. Police Scotland said inquiries were continuing and that the pair were due to appear at Dumbarton sheriff court on Monday. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Attacks on facilities by both sides in the conflict this week threaten grave consequences for the global economyMiddle East crisis – live updatesThe escalating attacks on key oil and gas projects in the Middle East are expected to fuel a new phase of the ongoing conflict, with profound consequences for the world’s energy supplies and the global economy.The Iran regime has vowed to target a string of key energy infrastructure across the region after warning that an Israeli strike on a production facility for its largest gasfield at South Pars on Wednesday had ignited a “full-scale economic war”. ai_summary: China has been preparing for a global energy crisis
The country has huge reserves of oil and gas
China also has alternative energy sources like wind and solar
The US-Israel war on Iran has affected global energy supplies
China's preparations are seen as a strategic move to secure its energy supply
